failure_modes.text: Conjunctive optimism can make a complex plan look safer than it is because the chain of dependent conditions is overestimated.
failure_modes.mitigation: Calibrate by quantifying the joint probability of the dependent conditions; shock-test each link independently to see where the chain actually breaks.

failure_modes.text: Base-rate neglect can keep the team anchored in the inside view when the outside view is the only honest way to size aleatory risk.
failure_modes.mitigation: Anchor decisions to the outside view first — what happened historically in the reference class — before adjusting for the specifics of this case.

failure_modes.text: Overprecision can hide epistemic ignorance by treating fragile assumptions as if they were already earned facts.
failure_modes.mitigation: Run sensitivity analysis on every key assumption; ask 'what would you have to believe?' and check whether each belief has been tested or merely assumed.

premortem_questions.text: What would you have to believe?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What would you have to believe?

premortem_questions.text: Is the worst case bad enough?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Is the worst case bad enough?

premortem_questions.text: Would you be really shocked if the outcome landed outside the range you just gave?

heuristics.text: Classify uncertainty first so the team knows whether to run statistical sizing or discovery work.
heuristics.source_quote: different forms of decision-making and analysis are required

heuristics.text: Anchor to the outside view and reference classes before adjusting for the specifics of the inside view.
heuristics.source_quote: moving away from an inside view ... towards anchoring decisions in the outside view

heuristics.text: Use simulations and scenario analysis when variability is real enough that one point estimate hides the true range of outcomes.

curation_notes.summary: The intervention layer stays on classifying uncertainty correctly, outside-view calibration, and explicit uncertainty-range checks.
donor_drops: Did not preserve broad uncertainty taxonomy sprawl because the raw source is strongest on tool choice, shock testing, and outside-view discipline.
open_questions: The first heuristic compresses a longer source distinction into one action rule, but it remains directly grounded.

allies: The source is rich on probabilistic thinking, systems-thinking, falsifiability, and first-principles-thinking.
antagonists: The raw antagonists section names WYSIATI, availability-style hammer use, confirmation-bias, and outcome-bias.
structured_tensions: The source explicitly preserves tensions against commitment-bias, status-quo-bias, and confirmation-bias.
